title: WHY ZOOS ARE GOOD
content_html:
Why zoos are good

A.

In my view, it is perfectly possible for many species of animals living in zoos or wildlife parks to have a quality of life as high as, or higher than, in the wild. Animals in good zoos get a varied and high-quality diet with all the supplements required, and any illnesses they might have will be treated. Their movement might be somewhat restricted, but they have a safe environment in which to live, and they are spared bullying and social ostracism by others of their kind. They do not suffer from the threat or stress of predators, or the irritation and pain of parasites or injuries. The average captive animal will have a greater life expectancy compared with its wild counterpart, and will not die of drought, of starvation or in the jaws of a predator. A lot of very nasty things happen to truly ‘wild’ animals that simply don’t happen in good zoos, and to view a life that is ‘free’ as one that is automatically ‘good’ is, I think, an error. Furthermore, zoos serve several key purposes.

B.

Firstly, zoos aid conservation. Colossal numbers of species are becoming extinct across the world, and many more are increasingly threatened and therefore risk extinction. Moreover, some of these collapses have been sudden, dramatic and unexpected, or were simply discovered very late in the day. A species protected in captivity can be bred up to provide a reservoir population against a population crash or extinction in the wild. A good number of species only exist in captivity, with many of these living in zoos. Still more only exist in the wild because they have been reintroduced from zoos, or have wild populations that have been boosted by captive bred animals. Without these efforts there would be fewer species alive today. Although reintroduction successes are few and far between, the numbers are increasing, and the very fact that species have been saved or reintroduced as a result of captive breeding proves the value of such initiatives.

C.

Zoos also provide education. Many children and adults, especially those in cities, will never see a wild animal beyond a fox or pigeon. While it is true that television documentaries are becoming ever more detailed and impressive, and many natural history specimens are on display in museums, there really is nothing to compare with seeing a living creature in the flesh, hearing it, smelling it, watching what it does and having the time to absorb details. That alone will bring a greater understanding and perspective to many, and hopefully give them a greater appreciation for wildlife, conservation efforts and how they can contribute.

D.

In addition to this, there is also the education that can take place in zoos through signs, talks and presentations which directly communicate information to visitors about the animals they are seeing and their place in the world. This was an area where zoos used to be lacking, but they are now increasingly sophisticated in their communication and outreach work. Many zoos also work directly to educate conservation workers in other countries, or send their animal keepers abroad to contribute their knowledge and skills to those working in zoos and reserves, thereby helping to improve conditions and reintroductions all over the world.

E.

Zoos also play a key role in research. If we are to save wild species and restore and repair ecosystems we need to know about how key species live, act and react. Being able to undertake research on animals in zoos where there is less risk and fewer variables means real changes can be effected on wild populations. Finding out about, for example, the oestrus cycle of an animal of its breeding rate helps us manage wild populations. Procedures such as capturing and moving at-risk or dangerous individuals are bolstered by knowledge gained in zoos about doses for anaesthetics, and by experience in handling and transporting animals. This can make a real difference to conservation efforts and to the reduction of human-animal conflicts, and can provide a knowledge base for helping with the increasing threats of habitat destruction and other problems.

F.

In conclusion, considering the many ongoing global threats to the environment, it is hard for me to see zoos as anything other than essential to the long-term survival of numerous species. They are vital not just in terms of protecting animals, but as a means of learning about them to aid those still in the wild, as well as educating and informing the general population about these animals and their world so that they can assist or at least accept the need to be more environmentally conscious. Without them, the world would be, and would increasingly become, a much poorer place

questions.groupHtml:
Questions 14–17

Reading Passage has sections A–G. Which section contains the following information?

Write the correct letter, A–G, in boxes 14–17 on your answer sheet.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword:
“how quickly / die out” / “species collapse / extinction fast”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn:
Paragraph B:
“some of these collapses have been sudden, dramatic and unexpected”
Bước 3: Phân tích:
“sudden” + “dramatic” = xảy ra rất nhanh → nói trực tiếp về tốc độ tuyệt chủng / sụp đổ quần thể loài.
=> Đáp án: B

_blockConfig.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.
featureStatements: A reference to how quickly animal species can die out
Reasons why it is preferable to study animals in captivity rather than in the wild
Mention of two ways of learning about animals other than visiting them in zoos
Reasons why animals in zoos may be healthier than those in the wild

questions.question_text: a reference to how quickly animal species can die out

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword:
“preferable / better study in captivity / wild”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn:
Paragraph E: “less risk and fewer variables means real changes can be effected…”
Bước 3: Phân tích:
Nghiên cứu trong sở thú:
ít rủi ro hơn
ít biến số hơn
→ dễ nghiên cứu hơn so với ngoài tự nhiên → lý do “preferable”
=> Đáp án: E
questions.question_text: reasons why it is preferable to study animals in captivity rather than in the wild

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword:
“other than zoos / other ways of learning animals”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn:
Paragraph C: “television documentaries… natural history specimens are on display in museums”
Bước 3: Phân tích:
Có 2 cách học về động vật ngoài sở thú:
TV documentaries
museum specimens
=> Đáp án: C
questions.question_text: mention of two ways of learning about animals other than visiting them in zoos

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword:
“healthier in zoos / better condition / wild”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn:
Paragraph A: “varied and high-quality diet… illnesses treated… no predators… no parasites…”
Bước 3: Phân tích:
Động vật trong sở thú khỏe hơn vì:
ăn tốt hơn
được chữa bệnh
không bị săn mồi
không ký sinh trùng / chấn thương
=> Đáp án: A
questions.question_text: reasons why animals in zoos may be healthier than those in the wild

questions.groupHtml:
Questions 18–22

Do the following statements agree with the information given in the Reading Passage?

In boxes 18–22 on your answer sheet, write
TRUE  if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE  if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN  if there is no information on this
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword:
“live longer / zoo vs wild / life expectancy”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn:
Paragraph A: “The average captive animal will have a greater life expectancy compared with its wild counterpart”
Bước 3: Phân tích:
“greater life expectancy” = sống lâu hơn → khẳng định trực tiếp so sánh zoo > wild
=> Đáp án: TRUE

_blockConfig.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.question_text: An animal is likely to live longer in a zoo than in the wild.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword:
“only in zoos / not in wild / extinct in wild”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn:
Paragraph B: “A good number of species only exist in captivity”
Bước 3: Phân tích:
“only exist in captivity” = chỉ còn trong sở thú → không còn ngoài tự nhiên
=> Đáp án: TRUE
questions.question_text: There are some species in zoos which can no longer be found in the wild.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword:
“TV documentaries / increase zoo visitors”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn:
Paragraph C: “television documentaries are becoming ever more detailed and impressive…”
Bước 3: Phân tích:
Bài chỉ nói:
TV tài liệu tốt hơn
giúp hiểu động vật hơn
❌ không nói tăng lượng khách đến zoo
=> Đáp án: NOT GIVEN
questions.question_text: Improvements in the quality of TV wildlife documentaries have resulted in increased numbers of zoo visitors.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword:
“always excelled / zoo education / communication”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn:
Paragraph D: “This was an area where zoos used to be lacking”
Bước 3: Phân tích:
“used to be lacking” = trước đây làm không tốt
→ trái với “always excelled”
=> Đáp án: FALSE
questions.question_text: Zoos have always excelled at transmitting information about animals to the public.

questions.explanation:
“less stressful / zoo vs wild / animals stress”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn:
Paragraph E: “less risk and fewer variables…”
Bước 3: Phân tích:
Bài nói:
ít rủi ro cho nghiên cứu
ít biến số
không nói ít stress cho động vật
=> Đáp án: NOT GIVEN
questions.question_text: Studying animals in zoos is less stressful for the animals than studying them in the wild.

questions.text: Which TWO of the following are stated about zoo staff in the text?
options: A. Some take part in television documentaries about animals
B. Some travel to overseas locations to join teams in zoos.
C. Some get experience with species in the wild before taking up zoo jobs.
D. Some teach people who are involved with conservation projects.
E. Some specialise in caring for species which are under threat.
questions.question: Which TWO of the following are stated about zoo staff in the text?
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword:
“zoo staff / overseas / travel abroad”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn:
Paragraph D: “send their animal keepers abroad to contribute their knowledge and skills…”
Bước 3: Phân tích:
“send… abroad” = cử nhân viên đi nước ngoài → đúng ý “travel overseas to work in teams”
=> Đáp án: B

_blockConfig.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.text: Which TWO of the following are stated about zoo staff in the text?
questions.question: Which TWO of the following are stated about zoo staff in the text?
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword:
“zoo staff / teach / conservation workers”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn:
Paragraph D:
“educate conservation workers in other countries”
Bước 3: Phân tích:
“educate conservation workers” = đào tạo người làm bảo tồn → đúng ý “teach conservation staff”
=> Đáp án: D

questions.text: Which TWO of these beliefs about zoos does the writer mention in the text?
options: A. They can help children overcome their fears of wild animals.
B. They can increase public awareness of environmental issues.
C. They can provide employment for a range of professional people.
D. They can generate income to support wildlife conservation projects.
E. They can raise animals which can later be released into the wild.
questions.question: Which TWO of these beliefs about zoos does the writer mention in the text?
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword:
“public awareness / environment / educate people”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn:
Paragraph C + F: “greater appreciation for wildlife, conservation efforts…”
“more environmentally conscious”
Bước 3: Phân tích:
Zoos giúp:
nâng nhận thức về động vật
khiến con người quan tâm môi trường hơn
→ đúng “increase awareness”
=> Đáp án: B

_blockConfig.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.question_text: Choose TWO Letters — Beliefs about zoos

questions.text: Which TWO of these beliefs about zoos does the writer mention in the text?
questions.question: Which TWO of these beliefs about zoos does the writer mention in the text?
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword:
“reintroduce / release into wild / captive breeding”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn:
Paragraph B:
“reintroduced from zoos… boosted by captive bred animals”
Bước 3: Phân tích:
“reintroduced” = thả lại tự nhiên → đúng ý nuôi rồi thả
=> Đáp án: E
questions.question_text: Choose TWO Letters — Beliefs about zoos
